docs.title: BroadPy Documentation
docs.text: broadpy provides fast, lightweight tools for spectral-line broadening: - instrumental broadening (Gaussian, Lorentzian, Voigt, variable Gaussian) - rotational broadening - JWST NIRSpec broadening using polynomial resolution curves

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
1. Install the package: see installation.md
  2. Run the example notebook: instrumental_broadening_examples.ipynb

docs.title: Example Notebook
docs.text:
The docs notebook includes: - constant-resolution instrumental broadening - variable-resolution broadening with linearly increasing R(λ) - JWST NIRSpec broadening (g140h) - comparison plots and residual panels

Open it directly from the repository root:

jupyter lab docs/instrumental_broadening_examples.ipynb

docs.title: Option 1: Install from source (recommended)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DGonzalezPicos/broadpy
cd broadpy
pip install .

docs.title: Option 2: Conda environment
docs.text:
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate broadpy-env
pip install .

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text: python -c "from broadpy import InstrumentalBroadening, RotationalBroadening; print('broadpy import OK')"

docs.title: Instrumental Broadening Quickstart
docs.text:
In [ ]: Copied! 
from __future__ import annotations

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from broadpy import InstrumentalBroadening
from broadpy.nirspec import NIRSPec, nirspec_broadening
from broadpy.utils import load_example_data
%load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2

SPEED_OF_LIGHT_KM_S = 2.99792458e5  # CODATA 2018 exact speed of light.
from __future__ import annotations import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt from broadpy import InstrumentalBroadening from broadpy.nirspec import NIRSPec, nirspec_broadening from broadpy.utils import load_example_data %load_ext autoreload %autoreload 2 SPEED_OF_LIGHT_KM_S = 2.99792458e5 # CODATA 2018 exact speed of light. 
The autoreload extension is already loaded. To reload it, use:
  %reload_ext autoreload
In [ ]: Copied! 
# Load a JWST-like example spectrum in the NIRSpec G140H range.
wavelength_nm, flux = load_example_data(wave_range=(1000.0, 1600.0), jwst=True)
flux /= np.nanmedian(flux)
# 1) Constant-resolution broadening.
resolution_constant = 2700.0
instrument = InstrumentalBroadening(wavelength_nm, flux)
flux_constant_resolution = instrument(resolution=resolution_constant, kernel="gaussian")

# 2) JWST NIRSpec broadening.
flux_nirspec = nirspec_broadening(wavelength_nm, flux, grating="g140h")

residual = flux_nirspec - flux_constant_resolution

fig, (ax_resolution, ax_spectrum, ax_residuals) = plt.subplots(
    3,
    1,
    figsize=(14, 9),
    sharex=True,
    gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [1, 3, 1]},
)

ax_resolution.plot(
    wavelength_nm,
    np.full_like(wavelength_nm, resolution_constant),
    label=f"Constant R={resolution_constant:.0f}",
)

# Effective R(lambda) used by the NIRSpec polynomial model.
resolution_nirspec = np.polyval(nirspec_model.poly_coefficients["g140h"], wavelength_nm)
ax_resolution.plot(wavelength_nm, resolution_nirspec, label="NIRSpec R(lambda)")
ax_resolution.set_title("(a) Resolution models")
ax_resolution.set_ylabel(r"R = $\lambda/\Delta\lambda$")
ax_resolution.legend(loc="best")

ax_spectrum.plot(
    wavelength_nm,
    flux_constant_resolution,
    linewidth=1.2,
    label=f"Constant R={resolution_constant:.0f}",
)

ax_spectrum.plot(
    wavelength_nm,
    flux_nirspec,
    linewidth=1.2,
    label="JWST NIRSpec (G140H)",
)
ax_spectrum.set_title("(b) Broadened spectra")
ax_spectrum.set_ylabel("Flux")
ax_spectrum.legend(loc="best")

ax_residuals.plot(wavelength_nm, residual, linewidth=1.0, label=r"NIRSpec R($\lambda$) - constant R")
ax_residuals.axhline(0.0, color="black", linestyle="-", linewidth=0.8)
ax_residuals.set_title("(c) Residuals")
ax_residuals.set_xlabel("Wavelength [nm]")
ax_residuals.set_ylabel("Residual flux")
ax_residuals.legend(loc="best")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
# Load a JWST-like example spectrum in the NIRSpec G140H range. wavelength_nm, flux = load_example_data(wave_range=(1000.0, 1600.0), jwst=True) flux /= np.nanmedian(flux) # 1) Constant-resolution broadening. resolution_constant = 2700.0 instrument = InstrumentalBroadening(wavelength_nm, flux) flux_constant_resolution = instrument(resolution=resolution_constant, kernel="gaussian") # 2) JWST NIRSpec broadening. flux_nirspec = nirspec_broadening(wavelength_nm, flux, grating="g140h") residual = flux_nirspec - flux_constant_resolution fig, (ax_resolution, ax_spectrum, ax_residuals) = plt.subplots( 3, 1, figsize=(14, 9), sharex=True, gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [1, 3, 1]}, ) ax_resolution.plot( wavelength_nm, np.full_like(wavelength_nm, resolution_constant), label=f"Constant R={resolution_constant:.0f}", ) # Effective R(lambda) used by the NIRSpec polynomial model. resolution_nirspec = np.polyval(nirspec_model.poly_coefficients["g140h"], wavelength_nm) ax_resolution.plot(wavelength_nm, resolution_nirspec, label="NIRSpec R(lambda)") ax_resolution.set_title("(a) Resolution models") ax_resolution.set_ylabel(r"R = $\lambda/\Delta\lambda$") ax_resolution.legend(loc="best") ax_spectrum.plot( wavelength_nm, flux_constant_resolution, linewidth=1.2, label=f"Constant R={resolution_constant:.0f}", ) ax_spectrum.plot( wavelength_nm, flux_nirspec, linewidth=1.2, label="JWST NIRSpec (G140H)", ) ax_spectrum.set_title("(b) Broadened spectra") ax_spectrum.set_ylabel("Flux") ax_spectrum.legend(loc="best") ax_residuals.plot(wavelength_nm, residual, linewidth=1.0, label=r"NIRSpec R($\lambda$) - constant R") ax_residuals.axhline(0.0, color="black", linestyle="-", linewidth=0.8) ax_residuals.set_title("(c) Residuals") ax_residuals.set_xlabel("Wavelength [nm]") ax_residuals.set_ylabel("Residual flux") ax_residuals.legend(loc="best") plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [33]: Copied! 
# Load and plot JWST NIRSpec resolution profiles.

fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 4))

for grating in NIRSPec.available_gratings:
    wavelength_range = NIRSPec.wavelength_range_gratings[grating]
    wavelength_grating = np.linspace(wavelength_range[0], wavelength_range[1], 1000)
    resolution = NIRSPec(wavelength=wavelength_grating).get_resolution_curve(grating=grating)
    ax.plot(wavelength_grating, resolution, linewidth=1.5, label=grating)

ax.set_xlabel("Wavelength [nm]")
ax.set_ylabel(r"Resolution $R = \lambda/\Delta\lambda$")
ax.set_title("JWST NIRSpec resolution profiles")
ax.legend(loc="best")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
# Load and plot JWST NIRSpec resolution profiles. fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 4)) for grating in NIRSPec.available_gratings: wavelength_range = NIRSPec.wavelength_range_gratings[grating] wavelength_grating = np.linspace(wavelength_range[0], wavelength_range[1], 1000) resolution = NIRSPec(wavelength=wavelength_grating).get_resolution_curve(grating=grating) ax.plot(wavelength_grating, resolution, linewidth=1.5, label=grating) ax.set_xlabel("Wavelength [nm]") ax.set_ylabel(r"Resolution $R = \lambda/\Delta\lambda$") ax.set_title("JWST NIRSpec resolution profiles") ax.legend(loc="best") plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [ ]: Copied!

docs.title: Instrumental Broadening Quickstart¶
docs.text:
This quickstart compares two instrumental broadening models on the same spectrum:

  1. Constant resolving power (R = constant)
  2. JWST NIRSpec broadening (R=R(wavelength))

docs.title: Gaussian kernel for instrumental broadening¶
docs.text:
The normalized Gaussian kernel is

$$ K(x) = \exp\left(-\frac{x^2}{2\sigma_{\rm pix}^2}\right), \qquad K \leftarrow \frac{K}{\sum K}, $$

where $\sigma_{\rm pix}$ is the kernel width in pixels.

For a resolving-power profile (R($\lambda$)), broadpy converts to velocity FWHM using

$$ v_{\rm FWHM}(\lambda) = \frac{c}{R(\lambda)}, $$

then to pixel sigma (on the internal log-wavelength sampling) with

$$ \sigma_{\rm pix}(\lambda) = \frac{v_{\rm FWHM}(\lambda)}{c\,\sqrt{8\ln 2}\,\Delta\ln\lambda}. $$

For variable broadening, you pass a vector fwhm (km/s), and InstrumentalBroadening(..., kernel="gaussian_variable") applies a local Gaussian kernel at each wavelength sample.
